gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/164)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/164)
text: شَكَوْتُ إِلَى أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) الْوَجَعَ فَقَالَ لِي إِذَا أَوَيْتَ إِلَى فِرَاشِكَ فَكُلْ سُكَّرَتَيْنِ قَالَ فَفَعَلْتُ ذَلِكَ فَبَرَأْتُ فَخَبَّرْتُ بَعْضَ الْمُتَطَبِّبِينَ وَ كَانَ أَفْرَهَ أَهْلِ بِلَادِنَا فَقَالَ مِنْ أَيْنَ عَرَفَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) هَذَا هَذَا مِنْ مَخْزُونِ عِلْمِنَا أَمَا إِنَّهُ صَاحِبُ كُتُبٍ فَيَنْبَغِي أَنْ يَكُونَ أَصَابَهُ فِي بَعْضِ كُتُبِهِ .

en.hubeali: Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hassan Bin Ali Bin Al no’man, from one of our companions who said,

‘I complained to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> of the pain. So he<sup>asws</sup> said to me: ‘When you go to your bed so eat two (lumps of) sugar’. He said, ‘So I did that, and I was cured. So I informed one of the physicians and he was the most splendid of the people of our city, so he said, ‘From where did Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> recognise this. This is from the treasures of our knowledge. However, he<sup>asws</sup> is the owner of Books, so it is befitting that he<sup>asws</sup> happened to have found it in one of his<sup>asws</sup> Books’.
en.sarwar: 5. Ahmad ibn Muhammad has narrated from al-Hassan ibn Ali ibn al-Nu’man from certain persons of our people who has said the following: “I once complained before abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, against pain and he (the Imam) said, ‘When you lie down in your bed eat two pieces of sugar.’ He (the narrator) has said, ‘I followed the instruction and I became well. I told a physician about it, which was the best in our town. He asked me, ‘How has abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, learned about it? It is of our secret knowledge. However he owns books, so he may have found it in his books.’”
